title: Queen Mary's College Important Dates 2026

detail.title: Queen Mary's College Important Dates 2026
detail.short_info: Queen Mary's College Application Form 2026 is now open for UG and PG admissions. Apply online through TNGASA portal for BA, B.Sc, B.Com, BCA, MA, M.Sc & M.Com courses at QMC Chennai. Check eligibility, important dates, merit list & counselling details for 2026.

content.text: Queen Mary's College (Autonomous), Chennai (QMC Chennai) has opened the application process for 2026 admissions primarily through the centralized Tamil Nadu Government Arts and Science Colleges Admission (TNGASA) portal. Women candidates can apply online for various UG and PG courses based on merit and counselling.

sections.heading: Queen Mary's College Application Form 2026 Overview

content.text: Queen Mary's College (Autonomous), Chennai, affiliated to the University of Madras and accredited with NAAC 'A+' grade (62nd in NIRF 2025), offers admissions to UG (BA, B.Sc, B.Com, BCA) and PG courses. Admissions for most UG programs occur through the TNGASA portal, emphasizing merit from qualifying exams (HSC for UG) followed by counselling. Only women candidates are eligible.
Note: Dates are based on available 2026 updates; always verify on the official TNGASA portal for exact notifications.

rows: Women candidates only; Passed HSC (10+2) or equivalent with relevant subjects; Age not exceeding 24 years as on July 1 (2-year relaxation for Physically Challenged)
Completed relevant UG degree from University of Madras or equivalent
Bachelor’s degree with Mathematics at 10+2 level; Valid TANCET score

content.text: The college follows Tamil Nadu Government reservation norms.

Application fees are typically handled through the centralized TNGASA portal. Specific fee details (including category-wise) are mentioned in the official prospectus on tngasa.in. No separate college fee is charged at the application stage for most UG programs.

sections.heading: How to Apply for Queen Mary's College Application Form 2026 (Step-by-Step)

items: Visit the official TNGASA portal: www.tngasa.in.
Register with valid details and fill the online application form.
Select Queen Mary's College and preferred courses.
Upload required documents and pay the application fee (if applicable).
Submit and download the confirmation. Track merit lists and counselling schedules on the college website or TNGASA portal.

sections.heading: Queen Mary's College Admission Process & Selection Criteria

content.text: Admissions are merit-based on HSC marks (Part-III total out of 400 for UG) for most courses, followed by counselling. Rank lists are published on the college website. MCA requires TANCET scores. Special quotas exist for Physically Challenged, Sports, Ex-Servicemen, etc. Shift I and II admissions occur on the same day.
